businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker expanding into autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips as a 'Physical AI Platform' company
businessSummary.description: Tesla goes beyond EV sales with FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue. Key catalysts are lined up for April 2026: Cybercab production start, Roadster unveiling, and more.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely elevated vs S&P 500 avg (~24x) and auto sector avg (~12x). AI/robotics/autonomy premium priced in, but needs earnings to back it up

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Still high but half of TTM, reflecting market expectations of earnings recovery

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 2.93% YoY. UBS cut Q1 delivery forecast to 345K, raising doubts about revenue recovery

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: At traditional OEM levels due to pricing pressure. Energy expansion and FSD subscriptions are key to margin improvement

keyMetrics.name: Free Cash Flow
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation after $8.53B capex. Ample capacity for Cybercab and Terafab investments

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash position of $29.3B. Top-tier balance sheet strength

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B Joint Chip Factory Launch (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI officially launched the Terafab semiconductor factory in Austin. Targeting 1 terawatt of annual computing power by 2027, though experts question feasibility given only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel can operate at this scale

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous robotaxi. NHTSA approval in progress. Would open an entirely new revenue stream

growthDrivers.title: Roadster Unveiling in April
growthDrivers.description: Musk announced on March 17 that the Roadster will be unveiled next month, calling it 'the most insane thing ever.' Brand halo effect expected

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal
growthDrivers.description: Secured next-gen AI chip production deal with Samsung. Reducing NVIDIA dependency through in-house AI infrastructure

growthDrivers.title: FSD Subscription Model ($99/month)
growthDrivers.description: Transitioned from $12K one-time purchase to $99/month subscription. Maximizing recurring revenue from 8.4B miles of driving data

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surge (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: Jan-Feb 2026 China sales up 35% YoY. Continued growth in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS charging standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for chip self-sufficiency

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Impossible for competitors to replicate at this scale in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Even competitors like Ford and GM use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1, catching Tesla in global EV sales volume. Strong pricing advantage

competitors.name: Rivian
competitors.detail: Signed $1B+ robotaxi partnership with Uber (3/19). Planning 10K autonomous R2 vehicles in San Francisco and Miami by 2028

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: UBS Slashes Tesla Q1 Delivery Forecast to 345K, Skeptical on Robotaxi Hype
recentNews.significance: UBS analyst Joseph Spak cut Q1 delivery estimate to 345,000 (-7% vs consensus 371K). Maintained Sell rating with $352 target, warning robotaxi expectations may disappoint

recentNews.headline: Analyst Warns SpaceX Merger Could Erase $750B in Tesla Value, Stock Slips Premarket
recentNews.significance: Following SpaceX-xAI merger ($1.25T valuation), a potential three-way Tesla merger emerged. An analyst warned it could destroy $750B in Tesla shareholder value. Premarket down 2.57%

recentNews.headline: Terafab Feasibility Questioned: 'Only 3 Companies Can Produce Chips at This Scale'
recentNews.significance: Electrek called Terafab 'Battery Day redux.' Experts noted only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel have the capability for this scale

recentNews.headline: Terafab $20-25B Joint Factory Officially Launched
recentNews.significance: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI officially launched their joint chip factory in Austin. Targeting 1 terawatt annual computing power from 2027

recentNews.headline: Uber Invests $1B+ in Rivian for 50K Robotaxi Fleet
recentNews.significance: Rivian R2 autonomous vehicles to deploy 10K units in San Francisco and Miami by 2028. Direct Cybercab competitor entering the market

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS Joseph Spak maintains Sell at $352, cutting Q1 deliveries to 345K. Wedbush Daniel Ives holds $600 target, calling Tesla 'the biggest Physical AI beneficiary.' 10 of 34 analysts have Sell ratings, unusually high

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 364x is 15x the S&P 500 average (24x) and 30x the auto sector (12x). Any earnings miss could trigger a sharp selloff

risks.title: SpaceX Merger Risk Emerging
risks.description: After SpaceX-xAI merger, speculation about a three-way Tesla merger has surfaced. An analyst warns this could destroy $750B in Tesla shareholder value due to conflicts of interest and governance concerns

risks.title: Q1 Delivery Decline Concerns
risks.description: UBS cut Q1 forecast to 345K (-7% vs consensus 371K, -18% vs Q4's 421K). European boycotts and global economic slowdown are weighing on demand

risks.title: Robotaxi Competition Intensifying
risks.description: Uber invested $1B+ in Rivian for 50K robotaxi deployment by 2028. Waymo also expanding. Competition is heating up before Cybercab even enters the market

risks.title: Musk Legal Risk Escalating
risks.description: Jury found Musk liable for investor fraud in Twitter acquisition (3/20). DOGE controversy and boycott movement adding pressure

risks.title: Price Competition
risks.description: Margin pressure continues from Chinese competitors like BYD. Gross margin has declined to 18%

buyReasons.title: Physical AI Platform Transformation
buyReasons.rationale: Evolving beyond a pure EV company into autonomous driving, robotics, and AI chips. Success in any segment could trigger a valuation re-rating

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Cluster
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production start, Roadster unveiling, Terafab operations, Q1 earnings (4/21). Multiple catalysts concentrated in a short period

buyReasons.title: FSD Subscription Recurring Revenue
buyReasons.rationale: 8.4B miles of driving data powering FSD at $99/month. Continuous revenue generation beyond vehicle sales

buyReasons.title: Balance Sheet Fortress
buyReasons.rationale: Net cash of $29.3B, FCF of $6.2B. Ample financial capacity for large-scale investments

buyReasons.title: Technically Oversold
buyReasons.rationale: RSI at 31.42, near oversold. Down 17% from March highs, hitting 2026 lows. Technical bounce potential in this zone

overallOpinion: UBS cut Q1 delivery forecast to 345K, pouring cold water on Tesla's recovery narrative. Premarket down another 2.57%, hitting 2026 lows
SpaceX-xAI merger has sparked speculation about a three-way deal with Tesla. An analyst warns this could destroy $750B in shareholder value
Uber's $1B+ Rivian robotaxi partnership means Cybercab faces competition before it even hits the market
RSI at 31.42 near oversold, closing at $367.96. Below both 50-day ($398) and 200-day ($406) averages. Wait for April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings) before acting

investmentVerdict.summary: Headwinds are piling up: UBS delivery cut, SpaceX merger uncertainty, and Rivian robotaxi competition. RSI near oversold suggests a technical bounce is possible, but waiting for April catalysts (Cybercab production, Q1 earnings) before committing is the prudent approach.

sources.name: Business Insider
sources.description: UBS Q1 delivery forecast cut to 345K, $352 target

sources.name: Stocktwits
sources.description: SpaceX merger $750B value destruction warning, premarket decline

sources.name: TipRanks
sources.description: TSLA hits 2026 lows, continued premarket decline

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: Musk found liable in Twitter investor fraud case

x.hook: UBS cuts deliveries, SpaceX merger warning hits
x.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) UBS slashed Q1 deliveries to 345K (consensus 371K). SpaceX merger could erase $750B in value. $367.96, 2026 low.

1/4
thread:
Uber invested $1B+ in Rivian for 50K robotaxis by 2028. Cybercab hasn't even launched and competition is already fierce.

2/4

Terafab launched but experts doubt feasibility. Musk's Twitter verdict, NHTSA probe, P/E 364x. Headwinds are stacking up.

3/4

RSI 31.42 near oversold. Below 50-day ($398) and 200-day ($406). Target $389 (+5.7%). Wait for April catalysts.

4/4

threads.hook: UBS cuts deliveries, SpaceX merger warning hits
threads.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) $367.96, 2026 low

UBS slashed Q1 deliveries to 345K (-7% vs consensus). A SpaceX merger could erase $750B in Tesla shareholder value.

Uber invested $1B+ in Rivian for 50K robotaxis by 2028. Competition is heating up before Cybercab even launches. Terafab skepticism continues.

RSI 31.42 near oversold. Below 50-day ($398) and 200-day ($406). Target $389 (+5.7%). April will decide the direction.

empathy.hook: Tesla shareholders face 2026 lows as bad news piles up
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) $367.96, 2026 low. UBS cuts deliveries. SpaceX merger fears. It's been a rough stretch for holders.

1/4
thread:
Good news (Terafab, April catalysts) is getting buried under bad news. The frustration is completely understandable.

2/4

RSI at 31.42 suggests the market may have oversold. Bounces tend to happen from these levels.

3/4

April brings Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings. Target $389 (+5.7%). It's not time to give up yet.

4/4

empathy.hook: Tesla shareholders at 2026 lows, bad news piling up
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) $367.96, 2026 low

UBS delivery cut, SpaceX merger fears, Rivian robotaxi competition. It's been brutal. The good news keeps getting buried.

RSI 31.42 suggests the market may have pushed too hard. Oversold bounces are common.

April brings Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings. Target $389 (+5.7%). Hang in there. Not time to give up.
